prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a3f1c8d02b7e49f6","BT":2,"BW":3,"HF":0.42,"RF":0.28,"MA":2,"hour_entropy":2.31,"behavioral_consistency":0.18,"response_latency_variance":4.83,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.87,"error_recovery_pattern":0.04,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.83,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.48,"gas_price_precision":0.68,"rationale":"Advanced evasion profile: all five HasciDB indicators sit comfortably below flag thresholds (BT=2, BW=3, HF=0.42, RF=0.28, MA=2) while all eight AI-discriminator features are centered on human-side means (hour_entropy≈2.3, behavioral_consistency≈0.18, latency_var≈4.8) to defeat cross-axis and 13-feature classifiers simultaneously."}
